JavaScript tests for `Math.log` function edge cases, including NaN, negative numbers, zero, and infinity.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.10-001.js](15.08.02.10-001.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.log(NaN)` returns `NaN` using an assertion.
- **[15.08.02.10-002.js](15.08.02.10-002.js.md)**: Tests that the logarithm of a negative number returns NaN using Math.log.
- **[15.08.02.10-003.js](15.08.02.10-003.js.md)**: Tests that the logarithm of positive zero returns negative infinity.
- **[15.08.02.10-004.js](15.08.02.10-004.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.log(-0)` returns `-Infinity`.
- **[15.08.02.10-005.js](15.08.02.10-005.js.md)**: Tests if the reciprocal of Math.log(1) equals Infinity.
- **[15.08.02.10-006.js](15.08.02.10-006.js.md)**: Tests that the logarithm of infinity is infinity using JavaScript's Math.log function.
